reason:
Mnemonic Trick: Ass = गधा (Donkey). A donkey works hard all day, so Assiduous means 'Hardworking'.

• Diligent: Hardworking (Correct Synonym)
• Lethargic: Lazy/Inactive (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Hindrance: Obstruction/Barrier (Incorrect)
• Giant: Exceptionally large (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Ass = गधा (Donkey). Assiduous means 'Hardworking'. We need the exact opposite.

• Lazy: Inactive/Lethargic (Correct Antonym)
• Industrious & Laborious: Hardworking (Incorrect, these are synonyms)
• Plebiscite: A direct vote by all members of an electorate (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: S + nag (नाग/Snake). If a snake crosses your path, it creates an obstruction.

• Impediment: Obstruction/Hindrance (Correct Synonym)
• Boon & Comfort: A helpful thing or advantage (Incorrect, these are antonyms)
• Dwarf: A small mythological creature (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: S + nag (नाग/Snake). A snag is an obstruction or problem. The opposite is to help or clear the way.

• Assist: To help or aid (Correct Antonym)
• Hindrance & Obstruct: To create barriers (Incorrect, these are synonyms)
• Gaunt: Extremely thin and bony (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Pud (पड़) + gy (गई). She is so fat that she fell down (pud gyi) after walking a short distance.

• Corpulent: Fat/Stout/Chubby (Correct Synonym)
• Lanky, Slim, Gaunt: Extremely thin and skinny (Incorrect, these are antonyms)

Mnemonic Trick: Pud (पड़) + gy (गई). Pudgy means very fat or chubby. We need the opposite.

• Thin: Slim or lean (Correct Antonym)
• Chubby, Fat, Stout: Overweight or plump (Incorrect, these are synonyms)

Mnemonic Trick: Elf sounds like Elephant. Other animals look like dwarfs (बौना) compared to a giant elephant.

• Gnome: A dwarf or small mythical creature (Correct Synonym)
• Giant: A huge being (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Extravagant: Spending freely (Incorrect)
• Miserly: Stingy (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Elf sounds like Elephant. An elf is a dwarf. The opposite is someone huge.

• Giant: A huge, towering being (Correct Antonym)
• Dwarf: A small creature (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Careen & Bend: To lean or swerve (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: St (Stomach) + out (बाहर). His stomach is out because he has become very fat.

• Portly: Fat/Corpulent (Correct Synonym)
• Emaciated, Slender, Thin: Very skinny or weak (Incorrect, these are antonyms)

Mnemonic Trick: St (Stomach) + out (बाहर). Stout means heavy or fat. The opposite is painfully thin.

• Gaunt: Extremely thin and lean (Correct Antonym)
• Fat, Corpulent, Obese: Overweight (Incorrect, these are synonyms)

Mnemonic Trick: Mingy sounds like मुंजी (Miser). A 'munji' person is always a miser.

• Miserly: Stingy/Cheapskate (Correct Synonym)
• Extravagant: Spending too much money freely (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Straighten: To unbend (Incorrect)
• Swerve: To turn sharply (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Mingy sounds like मुंजी (Miser). The opposite of being a miser is spending freely.

• Extravagant: Spending heavily without care (Correct Antonym)
• Miserly: Stingy (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Stout: Fat/Portly (Incorrect)
• Lanky: Uncomfortably tall and thin (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Swerve sounds like सवेरे (Morning). Everyone bends their body while doing yoga in the morning.

• Careen: To bend, lean, or turn sharply (Correct Synonym)
• Straighten: Make straight (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Abruptly: Suddenly (Incorrect)
• Grave: Serious (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Swerve sounds like सवेरे (Morning). Swerve means to bend or deviate. The opposite is to keep straight.

• Straighten: To unbend or continue directly (Correct Antonym)
• Bend: To lean or careen (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Fierce: Aggressive/Ferocious (Incorrect)
• Obese: Fat/Adipose (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Adi (आधी/Half) + pose (पोज). He is so fat that only half his body fits into the camera pose.

• Obese: Corpulent/Fat/Stout (Correct Synonym)
• Emaciated, Gaunt, Thin: Very skinny and frail (Incorrect, these are antonyms)

Mnemonic Trick: Adi (आधी) + pose (पोज). Adipose means fat. The opposite is skinny.

• Thin: Slim or lean (Correct Antonym)
• Stout, Fat: Overweight (Incorrect, these are synonyms)
• Fierce: Aggressive or violent (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Feroci (फिरकी) + us (हमे). When a dictator becomes violent, they mock us (firki lete hain).

• Fierce: Aggressive, savage, or violent (Correct Synonym)
• Gentle: Mild or calm (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Slowly: Gradually (Incorrect)
• Agitated: Anxious or disturbed (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Feroci (फिरकी) + us (हमे). Ferocious means fierce or cruel. The opposite is mild and kind.

• Gentle: Kind, mild, and tender (Correct Antonym)
• Fierce: Aggressive (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Abruptly: Suddenly (Incorrect)
• Grave: Serious (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Gradua = ग्रेजुएशन (Graduation). The process of graduation happens slowly over multiple years.

• Slowly: Step by step, little by little (Correct Synonym)
• Abruptly: Suddenly and fast (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Serious: Grave or sober (Incorrect)
• Agitated: Disturbed (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Gradua = ग्रेजुएशन (Graduation). Gradually means slowly. The opposite is happening instantly.

• Abruptly: Suddenly and unexpectedly (Correct Antonym)
• Slowly: Step by step (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Bend: Swerve or curve (Incorrect)
• Straighten: Unbend (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: So (सो) + ber (बियर). When he becomes serious, he drinks beer and goes to sleep.

• Grave: Serious and solemn (Correct Synonym)
• Agitated: Disturbed or restless (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Plebiscite: A public vote/Suffrage (Incorrect)
• Halo: An aura or atmosphere (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: So (सो) + ber (बियर). Sober means calm, serious, and composed. The opposite is feeling restless.

• Agitated: Feeling or appearing troubled or nervous (Correct Antonym)
• Serious & Grave: Solemn (Incorrect, these are synonyms)
• Dismal: Depressing or sad (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Careen sounds like करीना (Kareena). Kareena bends her body gracefully while dancing.

• Swerve: To bend, turn, or lean sharply (Correct Synonym)
• Straighten: To unbend (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Atmosphere: The surrounding aura (Incorrect)
• Cheerful: Happy (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Careen sounds like करीना (Kareena). Careen means to bend or deviate. The opposite is to go straight.

• Straighten: To move without bending (Correct Antonym)
• Swerve: To lean or bend (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Cowardly: Lacking courage/Craven (Incorrect)
• Bold: Brave (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Suffr (सफर/Journey) + age (उम्र). After completing the 18-year journey of age, a person gets the right to vote.

• Vote: A plebiscite or franchise right (Correct Synonym)
• Haste: Hurry/Bustle (Incorrect)
• Laziness: Sluggishness (Incorrect)
• Merriment: Happiness/Glee (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Aura sounds like Nora (Fatehi). Her powerful dance completely changes the atmosphere of the room.

• Atmosphere: The surrounding vibe, mood, or halo (Correct Synonym)
• Woe: Unhappiness or sorrow (Incorrect)
• Disappear: Vanish (Incorrect)
• Emerge: To appear (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Glum sounds like गुलाम (Slave). A slave is always forced to work, so they are always sad.

• Dismal: Depressing, gloomy, or sad (Correct Synonym)
• Cheerful: Happy and optimistic (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Slavery: Bondage (Incorrect)
• Liberty: Freedom (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Glum sounds like गुलाम (Slave). Glum means sad or gloomy. The opposite is happy.

• Cheerful: Noticeably happy and optimistic (Correct Antonym)
• Dismal: Sad and depressing (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Criticize: To find fault (Incorrect)
• Praise: To compliment (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: C (सीता/Sita) + raven (रावण/Ravana). Ravana was so cowardly that he kidnapped Sita when Lord Ram was absent.

• Cowardly: Lacking courage (Correct Synonym)
• Brave & Bold: Courageous and fearless (Incorrect, these are antonyms)
• Melancholy: Sad/Lugubrious (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: C (सीता) + raven (रावण). Craven means cowardly. The opposite is courageous.

• Brave: Ready to face and endure danger; fearless (Correct Antonym)
• Cowardly: Timid (Incorrect, this is a synonym)
• Denial: Reject/Disavowal (Incorrect)
• Approval: Agreement (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Bus (बस/Bus) + tle (टल्ली/Drunk). A drunk person rushing to catch a bus always shows haste and hurry.

• Hurry: Haste or rapid movement (Correct Synonym)
• Laziness: Sluggishness or lack of activity (Incorrect, this is the antonym)
• Unusual: Bizarre (Incorrect)
• Ordinary: Normal (Incorrect)

Mnemonic Trick: Bus (बस) + tle (टल्ली). Bustle means moving with chaotic haste. The opposite is doing nothing.

• Laziness: The quality of being unwilling to work; extreme slowness (Correct Antonym)
• Haste & Hurry: Rapid motion (Incorrect, these are synonyms)
• Lucid: Clear/Articulate (Incorrect)
